SLV ETF Flow Signal
← Back to dashboard

SLV ETF Flow Signal

Approximate SLV flow monitor built from iShares Historical shares outstanding data. This is a research proxy, not official issuer holdings flow.

As of 2026-03-16

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Strong Outflow regime with an ETF flow signal of -1.52 and a 60-day z-score of -2.37, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Long liquidation, not short covering.
  • The signal is currently in a persistent Strong Outflow state, reflecting an acceleration of capital exit from physical-backed silver vehicles (1).
  • Conviction Band: High; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability: Stable; Threshold Proximity: Far; Revision Sensitivity: Low.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regime Classification: Flow intensity and duration are mapped to labels (e.g., Strong Outflow) based on the etf_flow_signal composite.
  • Momentum Tracking: z_accel measures the rate of change between 20-day and 60-day flow z-scores to identify trend exhaustion or acceleration.
  • Shock Detection: flow_shock flags anomalous daily liquidity events exceeding historical standard deviation thresholds.
  • SLV iShares Historical Data: Latest observation 2026-03-16 (Fresh).

Key Dynamics:

  • Main Drivers: The 60-day flow z-score (-2.37) indicates a severe long-term drawdown in investor interest, further evidenced by the 20-day z-score of -1.33 (2).
  • Stabilisation: The z_accel of -0.22 suggest the rate of liquidation is no longer accelerating but remains deeply entrenched in negative territory.
  • Conditional Invalidation: A sustained return to positive flow_shares (net creations) would signal the end of the liquidation phase.
  • Stability Assessment: The regime has been consistently "Strong Outflow" since March 10, indicating high persistence of the current bearish sentiment.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Continued downward pressure on silver prices as liquidation continues.
  • Most plausible upside risk: A sharp short-squeeze if outflows reverse abruptly, potentially triggered by a macro shock.
  • Most plausible downside risk: Acceleration of outflows if key technical price levels are breached, leading to forced redemption.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks). The signal tracks short-to-medium term liquidity flows.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium. While indicative of investor sentiment, ETF flows must be balanced against futures positioning (2).

Macro Relevance:

  • Macro dimension: Demand Impulse and Liquidity.
  • Economic mechanism: Net redemptions in SLV force the liquidation of physical underlying metal, increasing spot market supply and weighing on price.
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interaction: Typically leads or confirms shifts in speculative positioning monitored in broader market reports (1).

Regime Context:

  • Regime Persistence: This is a persistent regime following a transition from "Moderate Outflow" in early February.
  • Direction of change: Stabilising at extreme negative levels.

Model Limitations:

  • Flows are approximated from daily changes in shares outstanding and may lag secondary market trading activity.
  • Revision window cannot be assessed from provided content.

Data & References:

  • SLV iShares Flow Proxy (2026-03-16).
  • Most influential: z_flow_60d (-2.37) and etf_flow_signal (-1.52).
  • Public datasets: LBMA silver stocks and COMEX inventory levels would provide additional validation.

SLV ETF Flow

Approximate flow signal derived from changes in SLV shares outstanding and NAV per share.
